commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: feat(ETC-596): show "Remove from AI context" in structure context menu
commits.body:
When a block is already in pendingBlocks, the context menu shows
"Remove from AI context" instead of "Add to AI context".

Tests are heavily mocked due to getRightClickMenu's many singleton
dependencies. Consider extracting the AI context menu logic into a
smaller, independently testable function to reduce mock surface.

commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: feat(ETC-596): add dismiss button to block context pills
commits.body:
Tests assert removeBlockContext is called with the correct index
rather than verifying the DOM update, because the mocked store
isn't reactive ($state). A broader refactor of the test file's
mock pattern (e.g. .test.svelte.ts with $state) would enable
true behavioral assertions.

commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: chore(dx): symlink wp-env log files into .local/logs/
commits.body:
Adds bin/link-local.sh which resolves the wp-env install path and
creates symlinks for debug.log, etch/, and automatic-css/ directories.
Runs automatically as part of `bun run dev`, and on-demand via
`bun run link-local` (e.g. after wp-env destroy).

ETC-610.title: Add target functionality to Class Prop
ETC-610.description:
When the user adds a class prop inside the component editor, he should be able to see a target icon (same like with for example Text Prop).

Clicking on the target icon and selecting the element from canvas should inject the following code into the element's attributes in HTML.

```
class="{props.classProp}"
```

ETC-612.title: Clear attached images when starting a new conversation
ETC-612.description:
## Bug

When a user pastes or drops an image into the AI chat input and then clears the conversation, the pending image thumbnails persist in the input area. `clearMessages()` resets messages, block context, and the image cache, but does not reset `pendingImages`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [X] **When the conversation is cleared**, pending images are also cleared.

## Context

Discovered during ETC-596. Same class of issue that was fixed for `pendingBlocks` in that ticket.

ETC-596.title: Remove Block from AI Context
ETC-596.description:
## Acceptance Criteria

**Given that** a block is in AI context:

- [X] **When I dismiss a pill in the chat input area**, the block is removed from AI context.
- [X] **When I select "Remove from AI context"**, the block is removed from AI context.
- [X] **When the AI chat conversation is cleared**, the next message array is cleared (including context).
- [X] **When the message is sent to the AI,** the next message array is cleared (including context).

## Technical Context

No AiContextStore — we're manipulating the array of the next messages.

ETC-595.title: Display Context Pills in AI Chat
ETC-595.description:
Feedback — shows the user which blocks are in context. Read-only

## Acceptance Criteria

**Given that** the AI chat panel is open:

- [X] **When no blocks have been added**, no pills are displayed in the chat input area.
- [X] **When a block has been added to AI context**, a pill with the block's name appears in the chat input area.
- [X] **When multiple blocks have been added to AI context**, a pill for each block is displayed.
- [X] **When a block's name exceeds \~15 characters**, the pill label is truncated with an ellipsis.

## Technical Context

* Adds `name` to `BlockContext` type to the array of messages in the AI Panel
* Pills render in `AiChatContent.svelte`
* Name reactivity is free because the store holds block references, and `block.label` is `$state`
* No dismiss button in this ticket — pills are read-only

ETC-611.title: Passthrough Blocks not scrolling into view.
ETC-611.description:
In some scenarious scrolling into view does not work as intended and can be frustrating for users.
Should be an easy fix.

ETC-592.title: Add Class Property Instance Input
ETC-592.description:
The design shall follow the new design in Figma

The user sees class badges for applied styles

Default classes from the component definition are shown when no instance value is set

When the user sets instance classes, they replace the defaults entirely (no merge.. at least for now)

The user can add a class by typing in an input field (*It depends on the new design*)

The user can remove a class by clicking the remove button on a badge (*It depends on the new design*)

Adding or removing classes propagates the change to the canvas
